skill.name: db-handler
skill.description: Manage Drizzle ORM database schemas, table definitions, relations, indexes, and safe schema-change planning.
skill.summary: Guides Drizzle ORM schema design, relations, indexes, and database change workflows.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Most static external-command findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks around file paths, TypeScript examples, and ORM syntax. One true external-command risk remains: the skill instructs use of npx drizzle-kit push, which can directly modify a database schema without migration review. No evidence found for weak cryptography, reconnaissance intent, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Direct Database Schema Push Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use npx drizzle-kit push to sync schema changes directly to the database. This is a legitimate development workflow, but it can bypass reviewed migration files and apply destructive schema changes if used against the wrong database.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly named and tied to syncing schema changes directly to a database. The surrounding context is legitimate database tooling, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick External Command False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution hits, but the reviewed locations are Markdown inline code, fenced TypeScript examples, file paths, or ORM syntax. These are documentation patterns, not executable shell backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown instructions and examples, not Ruby source files or shell scripts. Visual inspection confirms the backticks format code references rather than execute commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Finding Not Supported
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3, but that line only contains the skill description. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is descriptive text about database schemas and Drizzle ORM. There is no cryptographic API or algorithm named at that location.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Not Supported
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md line 33, but the line advises avoiding breaking database changes without confirmation. No evidence found of host, process, network, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content concerns database change safety, not system inspection. No reconnaissance command or data collection instruction appears in that context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct Database Mutation Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow prefers npx drizzle-kit push for schema synchronization. Direct push commands should be limited to approved environments and reviewed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is concrete and directly changes database schema state. It is dangerous only if used without environment checks or review.

content.value_statement: Database schema changes can become inconsistent when tables, relations, indexes, and JSON fields are planned separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused workflow for Drizzle ORM schema design and review.
actual_capabilities: Creates Drizzle pgTable schema definitions grouped by domain.
Adds primary keys, timestamps, foreign keys, and relations.
Requires Zod schemas for JSONB columns.
Recommends indexes for foreign keys, filters, and sort columns.
Guides N+1 query prevention with relational queries or joins.
limitations: It is written for Drizzle ORM and PostgreSQL-style schemas.
It does not generate migration files by default.
It requires user confirmation for complex or breaking database changes.
It does not validate live database credentials or deployment environments.

use_cases.title: Design New Application Tables
use_cases.description: Plan tables with keys, timestamps, relations, JSONB validation, and indexes before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Schema Changes
use_cases.description: Check proposed column changes for nullable defaults, relation safety, and indexing needs.

use_cases.title: Improve Query Performance
use_cases.description: Identify N+1 query risks and replace loop-based fetching with relational queries or joins.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Table
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Drizzle ORM table for a new feature. Include an ID, timestamps, main columns, and any required indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Add Relations Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Add relations between these tables and include indexes for each foreign key. Ask me before any breaking change.

prompt_templates.title: Model JSONB Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a JSONB column for these settings. Include the Zod schema, Drizzle type, and validation considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Query Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this data access pattern for N+1 queries. Propose a Drizzle relational query or join-based alternative.

output_examples.input: Create tables for users and posts.
output: A domain-grouped schema plan with users and posts tables.
Foreign key relation guidance between posts and users.
Recommended indexes for joins, status filters, and created date sorting.

output_examples.input: Add account settings stored as JSON.
output: A recommendation to define a Zod schema before adding the JSONB column.
A typed JSONB field plan with validation boundaries.
Questions about defaults, nullability, and migration safety.

output_examples.input: Fix slow loading for users with posts.
output: Identification of loop-based fetching as an N+1 query risk.
A relational query or join strategy to load related records together.
Index recommendations for the relation and common filters.

best_practices: Confirm target database and environment before running schema sync commands.
Add indexes for foreign keys, frequent filters, and sort fields.
Use nullable columns or defaults when changing existing tables.
anti_patterns: Do not run direct schema pushes against production without explicit approval.
Do not store untyped JSONB data without a Zod schema.
Do not fetch related records inside loops when joins or relational queries can be used.

faq.question: What database tooling does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL-style schema definitions and relational query patterns.

faq.question: Does it create migration files?
faq.answer: No. The skill says not to generate migration files by default and prefers direct schema sync for development.

faq.question: Is it safe for production databases?
faq.answer: Use caution. Direct schema push commands should only run after environment checks and explicit approval.

faq.question: Can it help with indexes?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires indexes for foreign keys, frequent filter columns, and common sort columns.

faq.question: How does it handle JSONB columns?
faq.answer: It requires a Zod schema for JSONB data so the stored shape remains explicit and typed.

faq.question: Can it prevent N+1 queries?
faq.answer: It guides agents to use Drizzle relational queries or joins instead of fetching related data inside loops.
